1.description: Price informativeness across applications. Figure1shows how price informativeness varies with the level of trading costs (c) for the applications considered in SectionIII. To better illustrate the results, the value of price informativeness for each of the applications is normalized by the level of price informativeness in each application whenc=1. This figure graphically extends Theorem2to cases with multidimensional heterogeneity, since it shows that price informativeness can increase, decrease, or remain constant when trading costs increase. We use the following parameters for each of the applications. Institutional and retail investors:μ=0.7,τθ=1,τθ¯=1,τsI=1,γI=1.2, andγR=1.5. Informed and uninformed investors:μ=0.7andτθ¯=1. Elastic noise traders:μ=0.6,τθ¯=1,τsI=1, andγ=1.5. Classic noise traders:μ=0.4andτθN=0.25. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
